bulk_dataset: 9 human bulk PBMC samples (Finotello et al.)
recommended_tool: DWLS (best scRNA-seq ref method); RLR/FARDEEP for signature-based
notes: CRITICAL GAP: Brain/microglia tissue not evaluated. High relative error flagged at <5% fractions. Does not validate substate-level resolution. Plan Revision Rule TRIGGERED — conditionally cleared by Chu et al. 2022.

reference_dataset: Patient-derived GBM scRNA-seq (Neftel et al. GBM28)
notes: CONTEXT CAVEAT: Validation in GBM pseudo-bulk not real AD bulk. r=0.97 is upper-bound estimate. Substate resolution shown for M1/M2 (more distinct than homeostatic/DAM-2). Plan tool change: DWLS → BayesPrism. Plan Revision Rule conditionally cleared.

GAP: Reports relative surrogate variables only — no absolute DLPFC microglial % reported. Detectability floor derived indirectly from Mathys et al. (2019): ~2.7% microglial nuclei in DLPFC snRNA-seq. Biological ground truth ~5-10% (histology). Combined with BayesPrism 1% threshold: detection feasible.

reference_dataset: Zhang et al. 2016 mouse cerebral cortex (cross-species limitation)
bulk_dataset: ROSMAP DLPFC (155 AD / 86 Control) + Mayo (TCX/CBE) + MSBB (STG/FP/IFG/PHG)
ground_truth: LCM oligodendrocytes + Mathys et al. 2019 snRNA-seq
microglia_concordance_r: 0.588 (DEG direction concordance for microglia vs. Mathys snRNA-seq — NOT Pearson r)
recommended_tool: NR — ESHRD not a fraction estimator
notes: CRITICAL CAVEAT: ESHRD assigns DEGs to cell types by enrichment score — it does NOT estimate absolute cellular fractions. Cannot provide detectability floor. 58.8% DEG direction concordance with Mathys snRNA-seq for microglia = weak (41.2% discordant). Mouse reference (Zhang 2016) applied to human tissue is a cross-species limitation. ROSMAP DLPFC: 74% of microglial DEGs upregulated in AD — directional signal confirmed but no fraction data. Tier 2 only.

bulk_dataset: AHBA (3702 microarray / 6 normal donors) + MSBB (975 AD/control RNA-seq)
microglia_concordance_r: 0.17-0.19 (PCC microglia vs. AD severity in MSBB Table 1)
notes: KEY NUMBER DELIVERED: Microglial fraction ~5-10% in normal human cortex (Figure 4E); significantly increased in AD correlating with Braak/plaque scores (likely >10% severe AD). ROSMAP not used — MSBB only. PCC 0.17-0.19 for microglia is statistically significant but weak effect size. No substate resolution. Combined with BayesPrism 1% threshold: baseline 5-10% is well above detection limit. Detectability floor question CLOSED.
